Hey everybody, welcome back. This week we're going to talk about the holy grail of marketing and that's something that's called the super system. Really this is a game changer for anybody who deploys this. But before I show you how to do it and before we start taking a deeper dive into how to do it, I want to give you a word of warning. Order of warning number one is this involves technical stuff sort of. All the super system really is, is a bunch of email sequences that you built and you just squish them all together so that when somebody becomes a lead, they're constantly getting some kind of promotion that really is all that it is. So if when I'm diagramming this stuff out for you and I'm kind of explaining it, you're like, oh my gosh, that's a lot of work, sort of, but not really, especially if you consider thing number two about a super system. And thing number two about a super system is it's not meant to be something that you build all at once. So this week, I'm going to try to keep it pretty brief. And the reason why is I don't want you to feel like you've got to do everything I'm showing you this week, this week, right? Like my business depends on a super system. I'll show you how it works in a minute, but I didn't build the whole thing over the course of like a couple of days. You know, essentially what a super system is is you've got some campaigns that you've run and then you're like, oh, these work pretty good. I'm just going to keep running them. And anytime somebody enters into my funnel and becomes a lead, they're eventually going to be sent this campaign. And if you do that enough times, you'll have a situation where someone becomes a lead and they're constantly getting promotions from you and you could be like an outer space or something and it doesn't matter, your leads are going to constantly be seeing offers from you. And that's ultimately what a super system is. Now there's more to it, but that's it in a nutshell. All right. So you don't have to do it all at once, nor should you unless you're just like an overachiever or something. Thing number two or three, math is not an exact science here. Okay. So thing number three is that when you build this super system, it's like doing, you know, 10 pushups or something, which is unpleasant. So they say, I've never been able to do 10 in a row. That's ridiculous. Nobody could do that. But let's say hypothetically, someone could do 10 pushups in a row. You know, that's really not that much fun. And so building a super system is kind of like doing 10 pushups in a row or something. The difference is imagine that if like today you said, okay, I'm going to do 10 pushups in a row and it was, you know, as unpleasant as it may sound or whatever, unless you're a show off and you do that kind of thing regularly. But let's say you do 10 pushups in a row today. Metaphorically speaking, having a super system is like you do those 10 pushups today, but it's your body is going to have the effect as if you did those 10 pushups every hour, every day, forever. So it was like you do the work once, but then you keep getting the benefits over and over and over again. That's what a super system is. It's really, really cool. The reason the super system is important is because of this. All right. So I'm going to take you to the screen. So welcome to the computer whiteboard 2000 here. And let's make sure we got a good pen quality. I think we do. All right. So as you know, basically any internet sales process works like this, you know, at the highest level, you get a lead, all right. So sorry for my horrible writing. And then you sell them stuff. Okay. And as you know this from previous work, the thing in the middle is your sales process. Right. And so we talked about earlier, you know, like how to optimize sales processes and here's how to like really maximize the sales process for a webinar. Here's how to maximize the sales process for a loss leader. And we have all those checklists and stuff. So what happens with most people, all right, is they'll be like, okay, I have this thing and my sales process is a webinar and I want to maximize it. So therefore I'm going to get a lead. I'm going to have a click sequence and that's going to drive them to the webinar, you know, and then I might have a go finish sequence and that's going to make sure they see the pitch and then I'm going to have a countdown sequence. All right. And that's going to like get me more sales. And you know what? It will get you more sales. So like if you or your clients or whoever, if they're running webinars or, you know, I'm just using this as an example, sales process and like we install all of that stuff for them, especially if we do deadline stacking like I showed you before, then that's just going to take their business to a whole new level. However, okay, here's the thing about a super system. So most people when they're getting email, they're like on their phone, right? So they become our lead and let's say they're like super interested in the secret report that reveals the seven ways to get, you know, everything they've ever wanted in life immediately. All right. And so they opt in for the report and they're on their phone and they're responding to a social media app and they opt in and they read the report and they get to the download page and they click open and then they get a text message or then their order's ready at Starbucks or the light turns green. God forbid they're doing this while driving, but a lot of people do, you know, so their attention is so freaking fragmented that if we just rely on like one single, you know, maximize or even partially maximize sales process, A, we're going to do pretty okay, especially if we do the best we can with that sales process, but B, we are forgetting about the rule of damats and the rule of damats says this. You might have even heard me talk about it. The rule of damats state that the majority of people will not do what you want them to do when it comes to trying to sell stuff online. All right. So like the majority of people that we send from an ad to an opt-in page, for example, they're not going to opt in. The majority of people who opt in and they're supposed to go read a free report or watch a video presentation or whatever, they're not going to watch it. The majority of the people who do, they're not going to click to the order form. The majority of people who click to the order form, they're not going to give you their credit card number. The majority of the people who buy the main thing are not going to go on to buy the next thing. That's normal. I call that the rule of damats because we're like, damn it. I wish they would all do the thing I want them to do, but they don't. Most won't, right? And that's okay. That's what a super system is built to counter attack. All right. The purpose of the super system is to accept the rule of damats and understand that your prospect is just doing this all day, right? And they're not really paying that much attention and we have to be in front of them all the time. So when they are paying attention, they're able to see your offer and they're able to take advantage of it. It's really, really cool. So I'm going to walk you through how to do it right now. At the end of the day, here's the thing. If we look at the sales process, if we understand the thing in the middle between getting a lead and getting a sale, that's what drives the revenue. The more stuff we put in here, in this giant gap here between lead and sale, the more attempts that we put in here to make the sale, the more that's going to come out on the other side, the more sales you are going to get. All right. And so that's why we have a super system. We're going to start ads next week and I want your ads to work and you want your ads to work. And the secret to making ads work is to understand the nature and the psychology of the people that are responding to your ads. They can be extremely interested, but most of them aren't going to do what you want them to do because they're distracted. They're on their phones a lot of time. It's the internet for the love of Christmas. All right. So we got to have a super system to constantly be following up with them. Good news is not that hard to build, especially when you build it over time. All right. Cool. So now we can take a deeper dive and we will use a webinar framework, webinar sales process as my first illustration of how to do this. Cool. All right. See you in the next video.
